cards.title: Mental State Examination (MSE) — Full Framework

alerts.label: MSE structure — document ALL domains
alerts.text: The MSE is a cross-sectional snapshot of the patient's mental functioning at the time of examination.

rows: Grooming, dress, hygiene, age vs stated age, obvious abnormalities
Psychomotor activity, eye contact, rapport, agitation, retardation
Agitated, retarded, hyperactive, catatonic, cooperative, guarded
Rate, rhythm, volume, quantity, spontaneity, coherence
Pressured, poverty of speech, dysarthric, mutism, logorrhoea
"How would you describe your mood?" — patient's own words
Observed emotional expression, range, appropriateness to content
Flat, blunted, restricted, labile, dysphoric, euphoric, congruent/incongruent
How thoughts are organised and connected
Circumstantial, tangential, flight of ideas, loose associations, thought blocking, perseveration
What patient is thinking about — delusions, obsessions, preoccupations
Paranoid, grandiose, nihilistic, somatic delusions; obsessions; suicidal/homicidal ideation
Alert, orientated × 3, short-term memory intact, serial 7s, proverb interpretation
Awareness of illness and need for treatment
Full / partial / absent insight. Grade 1–6 scale.
"What would you do if you found a wallet?" Social and clinical judgement.

pearls.label: 🟡 Insight vs. judgement
pearls.text: Insight = patient's awareness that they have a mental illness. Judgement = ability to make sound decisions. They often correlate but are distinct — a patient can have full insight into their diagnosis but poor judgement about safety. Always assess both, as they directly determine capacity and treatment planning.

cards.title: PHQ-9 — Depression Screening & Scoring

alerts.label: PHQ-9 — Patient Health Questionnaire (9 items)
alerts.text: Ask about the past 2 weeks: 0=Not at all · 1=Several days · 2=More than half the days · 3=Nearly every day

rows: Little interest or pleasure in doing things
Trouble falling or staying asleep, or sleeping too much
Feeling tired or having little energy
Feeling bad about yourself — or that you are a failure
Trouble concentrating on things (e.g. reading, TV)
Thoughts that you would be better off dead, or of hurting yourself

pearls.label: 🟡 Q9 — always ask directly
pearls.text: If Q9 is scored ≥1, always follow up directly: "Are you having any thoughts of harming yourself or ending your life?" Asking does NOT increase risk — it opens the door. Assess intent, plan, means, and protective factors. Safe messaging: explore, listen, refer, remove means. PHQ-9 score alone never dictates treatment — clinical judgement and context are essential.

cards.title: Psychotic Disorders — Recognition & Basics

alerts.label: Psychosis is a medical emergency until proven otherwise
alerts.text: Always rule out organic causes first — delirium, encephalitis, substance intoxication.

pearls.label: 🟡 First Episode Psychosis
pearls.text: Duration of Untreated Psychosis (DUP) is the strongest predictor of long-term outcome — shorter DUP = better prognosis. Refer immediately to psychiatry. Antipsychotics should be started at the lowest effective dose. Involve family. Address substance use. Naturopathic role: sleep, nutrition, omega-3 support, reduce substance use — always as adjuncts under psychiatric supervision.

cards.title: Bipolar Disorder — Recognition & Naturopathic Adjunct

alerts.label: Bipolar Disorder — Medical Management Essential ★
alerts.text: Mood stabilisers (lithium, valproate, lamotrigine) and antipsychotics are the mainstay — never replace with naturopathic treatment alone. Naturopathic role: adjunct support, medication side effect management, lifestyle stabilisation.

rows: Type I = full mania; Type II = hypomania only

pearls.label: 🟡 Misdiagnosis Pearl
pearls.text: Bipolar disorder is misdiagnosed as unipolar depression in 40% of cases — average delay to correct diagnosis is 10 years. Red flags for bipolar in a "depressed" patient: family history of bipolar, onset before 25, hypomanic episodes after antidepressants (antidepressant-induced mania), seasonal pattern, psychotic features during depression, history of impulsive behaviour. Always screen with MDQ (Mood Disorder Questionnaire) before prescribing St John's Wort or stimulating supplements for depression.

steps.title: Omega-3 Fatty Acids
steps.text: EPA+DHA 2–4g/day — strongest naturopathic evidence for bipolar depression. EPA is the active anti-depressant component. Multiple RCTs show significant reduction in depressive (not manic) symptoms as adjunct to mood stabilisers. Start low, increase slowly.

steps.title: Circadian Stabilisation ★
steps.text: Irregular sleep-wake cycles are the #1 trigger for mood episodes. Prescribe: fixed sleep/wake times 7 days/week, morning bright light therapy (10min), no blue light after 8pm, avoid shift work and jet lag where possible. Social rhythm therapy (SRT) is evidence-based psychosocial intervention.

steps.title: Lithium Side Effect Support
steps.text: Lithium causes: tremor (Mg 400mg/day helps), polyuria/polydipsia (monitor renal function), hypothyroidism (check TSH 6-monthly, supplement if needed), weight gain (low-GI diet, exercise). Never advise stopping lithium — abrupt cessation triggers severe rebound mania.

steps.title: Avoid in Bipolar
steps.text: St John's Wort — can trigger mania in bipolar patients. High-dose stimulating adaptogens (Rhodiola, Ginseng) during euthymic/depressive phase — switch risk. Caffeine excess — disrupts sleep, lowers seizure threshold. Alcohol — destabilises mood, interacts with medications.

cards.title: ADHD — Assessment & Naturopathic Protocol

alerts.label: ADHD — Neurodevelopmental Disorder
alerts.text: ADHD is a neurodevelopmental condition with neurobiological basis (dopamine/noradrenaline dysregulation, frontal-striatal circuit differences). It is NOT caused by poor parenting or diet alone — but nutrition, sleep, and lifestyle significantly modulate symptom severity.

rows: Difficulty sustaining attention, easily distracted, forgetful, loses things
Often missed in girls — "daydreaming", academic underperformance
Fidgeting, inability to sit still, talks excessively, impulsive decisions
Classic presentation — often diagnosed in boys
Most adults with ADHD have combined type
Dopamine synthesis cofactor, methylphenidate response modulator
Dopamine synthesis — ferritin <30 worsens ADHD significantly
Remove: artificial colours, preservatives, gluten (if sensitive), dairy
★★★ Good — Southampton study confirmed food dyes worsen ADHD
★★★★ Strong — equivalent to low-dose methylphenidate acutely

pearls.label: 🟡 Ferritin & ADHD
pearls.text: Check serum ferritin in ALL children with ADHD before starting supplements. Ferritin <30 µg/L is associated with significantly worse ADHD symptoms — independent of anaemia. Iron is an essential cofactor for tyrosine hydroxylase (the rate-limiting enzyme in dopamine synthesis). Correcting iron deficiency alone improves ADHD scores comparably to low-dose stimulants in some children. Target ferritin >50 µg/L. Use iron bisglycinate — better absorbed, less GI upset than ferrous sulphate.

cards.title: Eating Disorders — Recognition & Naturopathic Support

alerts.label: Eating Disorders — Highest Mortality of Any Psychiatric Disorder ★
alerts.text: Anorexia nervosa has the highest mortality rate of any psychiatric condition (5–10%). Medical stabilisation and specialist psychiatric/dietitian care are ESSENTIAL — naturopathic role is adjunct support only. Never manage eating disorders without specialist team involvement.

rows: BMI <17.5, fear of weight gain, distorted body image, restriction
Cardiac arrhythmia (QTc prolongation), electrolyte imbalance, osteoporosis, refeeding syndrome
Nutritional rehabilitation support, bone health, REFER specialist
Binge-purge cycles, normal/near-normal weight, dental erosion, Russell's sign
Hypokalaemia (arrhythmia), oesophagitis, parotid enlargement, electrolyte imbalance
Electrolyte support, meal structure, trauma-informed counselling
Recurrent binge episodes without purging, significant distress, often obesity
Metabolic syndrome, T2DM, cardiovascular risk, depression
Mindful eating, emotional regulation, gut health, blood sugar stabilisation
Avoidant/restrictive food intake — sensory aversions, fear of choking/vomiting, not body image
Nutritional deficiencies, growth issues in children
Food introduction hierarchy, occupational therapy, nutritional supplementation

pearls.label: 🟡 Orthorexia — The Hidden Eating Disorder
pearls.text: Orthorexia nervosa (not yet in DSM) = obsessive focus on eating "correctly" or "healthily" — causes significant distress, nutritional deficiency, and social impairment. Common in health-conscious populations and IRONICALLY in patients seeking naturopathic care. Red flags: extreme food rules, anxiety when rules are broken, elimination of entire food groups without medical reason, social withdrawal around food. The naturopathic community has a responsibility to model a balanced, flexible relationship with food — not rigid dietary perfectionism.

steps.title: Red Flags — Refer Immediately
steps.text: BMI <15, rapid weight loss (>1kg/week), cardiac symptoms (palpitations, syncope, chest pain), electrolyte abnormalities (K <3.0, Na <130), refusal to eat/drink, severe depression or suicidal ideation. These require hospital admission — not outpatient naturopathic care.

steps.title: Nutritional Support in Recovery
steps.text: Zinc 30mg/day — deficiency worsens anorexia and appetite suppression (zinc reduces appetite in deficiency). Omega-3 3g/day — reduces depression and anxiety common in eating disorders. Vit D + Ca + K2 — critical for bone density (osteoporosis affects 50% of anorexia patients). B-complex for metabolic support during refeeding.

cards.title: Burnout — Clinical Assessment & Recovery Protocol

alerts.label: Burnout — WHO ICD-11 (2019)
alerts.text: Burnout is an occupational phenomenon — NOT a medical condition. Defined by 3 dimensions: (1) Emotional exhaustion — feeling depleted, drained. (2) Depersonalisation/cynicism — detachment, negative attitudes. (3) Reduced sense of accomplishment — feeling ineffective.

rows: Irritability, reduced efficiency, sleep disturbance, recurrent illness
Adaptogens, Mg, sleep priority, workload reduction
Chronic fatigue, cynicism, anxiety, physical symptoms (GI, headache, palpitations)
Complete exhaustion, detachment, cognitive impairment, depression overlap
Medical leave essential + full HPA rehabilitation

steps.title: Assessment Tools
steps.text: Maslach Burnout Inventory (MBI) — gold standard, 22 items, 3 subscales. Copenhagen Burnout Inventory (CBI) — free, validated. Distinguish from depression (PHQ-9): burnout is context-specific (work) and improves with vacation; depression is pervasive.

steps.title: HPA Rehabilitation Protocol
steps.text: Sleep 8–9hrs — non-negotiable first step. Ashwagandha KSM-66 300mg BD (cortisol ↓ in stage 1–2; adrenal support in stage 3–4). Rhodiola rosea 200–400mg AM (fatigue and cognitive performance — avoid in stage 4 low cortisol). Vit C 1–2g BD (adrenal cortex support). Pantothenic acid (B5) 500mg — cortisol synthesis cofactor. Magnesium glycinate 400mg night.

steps.title: Lifestyle Restructuring
steps.text: Work reduction — minimum 20% workload reduction in stage 3–4 (medical certificate if employed). Exercise: yoga and walking ONLY in stage 4 (HIIT worsens HPA). Nature exposure 2hrs/week (cortisol ↓ 12% — Shinrin-yoku). Digital detox — scheduled screen-free time. Restore social connection — isolation worsens burnout.

steps.title: Distinguish from Depression
steps.text: Key difference: in burnout, patient says "I have nothing left to give"; in depression, "I am nothing." Burnout → depression is a real progression — screen with PHQ-9 at every visit. If PHQ-9 >10, refer for psychological support. Burnout + depression = significantly worse prognosis without dual treatment.

cards.title: OCD, PTSD & Addiction — Naturopathic Adjuncts

alerts.label: OCD — Obsessions (ego-dystonic, unwanted) + Compulsions (>1hr/day or significant impairment)
alerts.text: ERP (Exposure and Response Prevention) = gold standard. SSRIs at higher doses. NAC 2400–3000mg/day (glutamate modulator — RCT evidence). Inositol 12–18g/day. NEVER provide repeated reassurance — it worsens OCD (reassurance IS a compulsion).

rows: Grounding techniques (5-4-3-2-1 senses), EMDR (refer), somatic experiencing
Extended exhalation breathing (4:6 or 4:8), HRV biofeedback, Mg glycinate 400mg night
Trauma-sensitive yoga ★ (van der Kolk RCT — significant symptom reduction), MBSR
Cognitive Processing Therapy (refer), Ashwagandha 300mg BD (cortisol ↓), Omega-3 3g
Medical supervision required — not naturopathic alone
NAC (reduces craving), Mg IV (alcohol withdrawal seizure prevention), B vitamins (thiamine URGENTLY in alcohol)
High-protein diet (neurotransmitter precursors), omega-3 3g, Mg 400mg, B-complex, Vit C 2g, NAC 600mg BD, probiotics (gut-brain axis repair)

pearls.label: 🟡 Grief vs Depression — Distinction
pearls.text: Normal grief: waves of sadness triggered by reminders, preserved self-esteem, short-lived low mood, person can experience positive emotions. Complicated grief (Prolonged Grief Disorder): yearning, difficulty accepting loss, bitterness, intrusive images >12 months — impairs functioning. Major depression: pervasive hopelessness, anhedonia, worthlessness, vegetative symptoms — requires antidepressant ± therapy. Social support, grief groups, narrative therapy, ritual, and meaning-making are the primary naturopathic tools for uncomplicated grief. Never pathologise normal grief.

🟡 Grief vs Depression — Distinction
Normal grief: waves of sadness triggered by reminders, preserved self-esteem, short-lived low mood, person can experience positive emotions. Complicated grief (Prolonged Grief Disorder): yearning, difficulty accepting loss, bitterness, intrusive images >12 months — impairs functioning. Major depression: pervasive hopelessness, anhedonia, worthlessness, vegetative symptoms — requires antidepressant ± therapy. Social support, grief groups, narrative therapy, ritual, and meaning-making are the primary naturopathic tools for uncomplicated grief. Never pathologise normal grief.

cards.title: OCD — Recognition & Naturopathic Adjunct

alerts.label: OCD — Key Distinction
alerts.text: OCD thoughts are ego-dystonic (patient knows they are irrational and unwanted — they cause distress). Anxiety thoughts are ego-syntonic (patient believes the concern is valid). This distinction determines treatment.

rows: Excessive hand washing, cleaning, avoiding perceived contamination
Harm (fear of harming self or others)
Checking (locks, gas, appliances), seeking reassurance repeatedly
Arranging objects, repeating actions until it feels "just right"
Mental rituals, prayer, avoidance of triggers
Inability to discard items regardless of value or condition

pearls.label: 🟡 Never Provide Reassurance in OCD
pearls.text: Reassurance WORSENS OCD — it is a form of compulsion that reinforces the cycle. In OCD, the therapeutic approach is the opposite of what patients want: "stop performing the compulsion even though the anxiety feels unbearable." This is ERP — habituation occurs when the compulsion is resisted. Do not ask "are you sure the door was locked?" alongside a patient — you are reinforcing their checking behaviour. Educate family members on this critical point.

steps.title: Evidence-Based Treatment — Refer
steps.text: ERP (Exposure and Response Prevention — CBT) is gold standard — superior to medication alone. SSRIs (fluoxetine, fluvoxamine, clomipramine) — effective in 60%, higher doses needed than for depression. Refer to OCD-specialist therapist — generic CBT therapists may not be trained in ERP.

steps.title: Naturopathic Adjunct
steps.text: NAC (N-Acetyl Cysteine) 2400-3000mg/day — glutamate modulator, reduces compulsive behaviours (RCT evidence for OCD and trichotillomania). Inositol 12-18g/day — competes with serotonin receptor signalling, comparable to fluvoxamine in one trial. Omega-3 3g/day — anti-neuroinflammatory. Exercise 30 min daily — serotonin and BDNF up.

pearls.label: 🟡 Supporting Bereaved Patients
pearls.text: What NOT to say: "They are in a better place", "Everything happens for a reason", "Be strong", "I know how you feel", "At least they had a good life." What TO say: "I am so sorry for your loss", "I cannot imagine how hard this must be", "Tell me about them." Practical help: offer specific tasks ("I will bring dinner Tuesday") rather than vague offers. Grief does not follow stages in order (Kubler-Ross stages are commonly misapplied). The naturopathic therapeutic relationship — consistent, compassionate, non-judgmental presence — is itself a healing intervention.

steps.title: Mind-Body Naturopathic Adjuncts

cards.title: Suicidality — Safe Assessment & Referral

alerts.label: ⚠️ Suicidality — BNYS Practitioners Have a Duty of Care
alerts.text: Every BNYS practitioner will encounter patients with suicidal thoughts. Asking about suicidality does NOT plant the idea — evidence shows it reduces distress and opens communication. Safe assessment identifies risk level and guides appropriate care. When in doubt: err on the side of safety and refer.

rows: Passive ideation ("I wish I wasn't here") — no plan, no intent, no means access, no recent attempt. Strong protective factors (family, faith, future plans).
Safety plan (written). Increase follow-up frequency. Refer to psychologist/psychiatrist. Remove obvious means (medications, weapons). Document.
Active ideation + vague plan OR clear plan without intent to act. Impulsive traits. Substance use. Recent loss.
Same-day psychiatry referral. Involve family with patient's consent. Safety plan. Consider voluntary hospitalisation. Document risk assessment thoroughly.
Active ideation + specific plan + means access + intent. Rehearsal behaviour. Recent attempt. Hopelessness. Social isolation.
EMERGENCY — call 108 or arrange direct psychiatric emergency. Do not leave patient alone. Ambulance or accompanied transfer. Document everything. Mandatory reporting obligations apply.

steps.title: Columbia Suicide Severity Rating Scale (CSSRS) — Screening Questions
steps.text: 1. Have you wished you were dead or wished you could go to sleep and not wake up? (Passive ideation). 2. Have you had thoughts of killing yourself? (Active ideation). 3. Have you thought about how you might do this? (Ideation with method). 4. Have you had any intention of acting on these thoughts? (Ideation with intent). 5. Have you started to work out the details of how to kill yourself? (Planned ideation). 6. Have you ever done anything, started to do anything, or prepared to do anything to end your life? (Behaviour — past or recent). Any "yes" to questions 3–6 = significant risk requiring immediate action. CSSRS is freely available in translated formats including Hindi.

steps.title: Safety Planning Intervention (SPI)
steps.text: Written safety plan (more effective than no-suicide contracts ★★★): 1. Warning signs I notice before a crisis (specific — e.g., "I stop eating, stay in bed all day"). 2. Internal coping strategies — things I can do alone to distract. 3. Social contacts who provide distraction (people, not yet sharing the problem). 4. People I can ask for help. 5. Professionals to contact in crisis (psychiatrist, helpline: iCall 9152987821, Vandrevala Foundation 1860-2662-345). 6. Means restriction — specific items to secure or remove. Patient keeps a copy. Provider keeps a copy. App: Safety Planning app (from the suicide prevention researchers who created SPI).

steps.title: India Mandated Reporting — Know Your Legal Obligations
steps.text: Mental Healthcare Act 2017 (India): Suicide is decriminalised in India (Section 309 IPC repealed). The MHA 2017 presumes a person who attempts suicide has severe stress — requires care and treatment, not criminal punishment. Mental health professionals: if a patient discloses suicidal plan with high lethality AND imminent danger, you may notify emergency services. Families: involve with patient consent when possible. Documentation: document every risk assessment conversation, decision made, rationale, actions taken, and follow-up plan. Good documentation protects both patient and practitioner.

cards.title: Autism Spectrum Disorder (ASD) — Naturopathic

alerts.label: BNYS Role in ASD — Supportive, Not Curative
alerts.text: ASD is a neurodevelopmental condition — not caused by vaccines (Andrew Wakefield's fraudulent 1998 paper was fully retracted in 2010). Naturopathic support targets: comorbidities (GI dysfunction, sleep disorders, anxiety, immune dysregulation) which significantly affect function and quality of life. Always work within a multidisciplinary team: developmental paediatrician, speech therapy, applied behaviour analysis (ABA), occupational therapy.

rows: Gut microbiome optimisation: probiotics (Lactobacillus rhamnosus + Bifidobacterium longum ★★). Dietary fibre (challenging — sensory issues with texture). Gluten-casein free diet: equivocal evidence overall, but subset with genuine food sensitivity shows significant behavioural improvement. Trial 3–6 months with dietitian.

Melatonin 0.5–5mg ★★★ (most evidence-based ASD supplement — Cochrane: significant improvement in sleep onset and total sleep time in ASD). Sleep hygiene protocol adapted for sensory needs: weighted blanket, consistent routine, blackout curtains, white noise.

Mind-body: yoga ★★ (adapted yoga for ASD improves anxiety, sensory processing, social interaction). Magnesium + B6 combination ★★ (Rimland protocol: high-dose B6 200–500mg + Mg 100–200mg — used extensively in ASD research). HRV biofeedback ★★.

Omega-3 DHA 1–2g/day ★★ (reduces hyperactivity and stereotypy in ASD RCTs). Vit D 2000–3000 IU (deficiency common — VDR dysfunction in ASD). NAC 900–1800mg/day ★★ (reduces irritability and repetitive behaviours in RCT).

pearls.label: 🟡 Sensory Processing in ASD — Practical Clinical Adjustments
pearls.text: ASD patients may have: hypersensitivity to touch (light touch more aversive than deep pressure — use firm handshake, not light touch). Auditory hypersensitivity (loud clinic environment may cause meltdown — quiet room, avoid paging systems during appointment). Visual sensitivity (fluorescent lighting may cause distress — use natural light or warm lighting). Many ASD patients prefer: consistent appointment timing and structure, advance notice of any change, written or visual instructions over verbal-only. Adjust your consultation environment and communication style accordingly. Parents/carers are invaluable informants — include them in every ASD consultation.

cards.title: Personality Disorders — Recognition

alerts.label: Personality Disorders in Naturopathic Practice
alerts.text: Personality disorders are pervasive, enduring patterns of inner experience and behaviour that deviate from cultural expectations, are inflexible, and cause distress or impairment. They significantly affect the therapeutic relationship, treatment adherence, and clinical outcomes. Recognition helps adapt communication and set appropriate boundaries.

rows: Paranoid: mistrust, suspicion of others' motives. Schizoid: detachment from relationships, restricted emotion. Schizotypal: eccentric behaviour, odd beliefs, perceptual distortions.
May be suspicious of medical interventions or perceive judgement where none exists. Require explicit, transparent communication. Avoid jargon.
Intense, unstable relationships, identity disturbance, impulsivity, self-harm, fear of abandonment, emotional dysregulation. "All-or-nothing" thinking (splitting).

Most clinically impactful: may idealise then devalue practitioner ("best doctor ever" → "you don't care about me"). Clear, consistent boundaries are ESSENTIAL. Refer to DBT-trained therapist. Document all consultations thoroughly.
Grandiosity, need for admiration, lack of empathy. Entitlement. Exploitative.
May demand special treatment, become angry when given standard care. Complaining and litigation risk higher. Maintain equanimity and professional distance.
Disregard for others' rights, deceit, impulsivity, aggressiveness, lawbreaking. Callousness.
May attempt to manipulate practitioner (seeking medications, false symptoms). Firm, non-judgmental boundaries. Do not be manipulated into inappropriate prescribing.
Avoidant: inhibition, sensitivity to criticism, feeling inadequate. Dependent: excessive need to be cared for, clinging. OCPD: preoccupation with order, perfectionism, control.
Avoidant: may avoid follow-up out of fear of evaluation. Dependent: may over-rely on practitioner, become distressed by boundaries. OCPD: may resist natural, "uncontrolled" approaches.

pearls.label: 🟡 BPD — The Most Important Cluster B for BNYS
pearls.text: Borderline personality disorder (BPD) is more common in clinical settings than general population (5–10% of outpatients). BNYS practitioners should: set clear boundaries at the beginning of the relationship (session length, communication channels, what constitutes an emergency). Use consistent, warm, non-reactive responses to splitting. Refer to: DBT (Dialectical Behaviour Therapy) specialist — the gold-standard treatment for BPD ★★★. ACT and schema therapy also effective. Never attempt to provide intensive psychotherapy within a naturopathic consultation — this creates unhealthy dependency and is outside BNYS scope. Having a named psychiatrist co-managing a BPD patient significantly reduces practitioner burden.

cards.title: Pharmacology of Psychiatric Drugs

alerts.label: Why BNYS Practitioners Need Psychiatric Drug Knowledge
alerts.text: Most BNYS patients are on or have been on psychiatric medications. Understanding mechanisms, side effects, nutritional interactions, and tapering principles enables: (1) Appropriate herb-drug interaction screening. (2) Managing medication side effects naturopathically. (3) Supporting patients who wish to taper under medical supervision. (4) Communicating effectively with prescribing physicians.

rows: GI nausea (first 2 weeks), sexual dysfunction (50%), insomnia or sedation, weight gain (paroxetine worst), discontinuation syndrome (especially paroxetine)

St John's Wort: ABSOLUTE contraindication (serotonin syndrome risk ★★★). Omega-3 ★★★ (augments SSRI in depression). Folate/methylfolate ★★★ (augments — reduces non-response). Magnesium (reduces side effects). Reduce antidepressant dose by 50% cautiously — always under medical supervision
Hypertension (↑ BP at higher doses — especially venlafaxine). Sexual dysfunction. Sweating. Difficult discontinuation (venlafaxine has severe discontinuation syndrome — must taper 10% per month)
Same cautions as SSRI. Duloxetine: also treats DPN and fibromyalgia. Never stop SNRIs abruptly.

Sedation, dry mouth, constipation, urinary retention, orthostatic hypotension, weight gain, cardiac arrhythmia (QT prolongation — dangerous in overdose → never prescribe large quantities to suicidal patients)
Avoid: anticholinergic herbs (belladonna). Alcohol potentiates sedation dramatically. CoQ10 (mitochondrial protection from TCA oxidative stress). TCAs deplete: CoQ10, B2, B12, folate.
EPS (extrapyramidal symptoms): acute dystonia, akathisia (inner restlessness), parkinsonism. Tardive dyskinesia with long-term use. NMS (neuroleptic malignant syndrome) = rare but fatal emergency.
CoQ10 200mg + Vit E 400IU (reduce EPS severity ★★). Ginkgo biloba 120mg reduces TD severity ★★. B6 (pyridoxine or P5P) 300mg reduces TD and akathisia ★★★ (strong evidence).

Metabolic syndrome (olanzapine/clozapine worst — weight gain 5–10kg, T2DM risk 3×, dyslipidaemia, HTN). Sedation. Prolactin elevation (risperidone). Agranulocytosis (clozapine — mandatory monitoring).

Clozapine + St John's Wort: ↓ clozapine levels (CYP3A4). Omega-3 3g (reduces metabolic side effects, augments risperidone ★★). Metformin (medical) for antipsychotic-induced metabolic syndrome. Berberine (naturopathic metabolic protection).
Varied: GSK3β inhibition (Li), Na channel (CBZ/LTG), GABA potentiation (VPA)

cards.title: Psychotic Disorders — Recognition & Basics

alerts.label: Psychosis is a medical emergency until proven otherwise
alerts.text: Always rule out organic causes first — delirium, encephalitis, substance intoxication.

pearls.label: 🟡 First Episode Psychosis
pearls.text: Duration of Untreated Psychosis (DUP) is the strongest predictor of long-term outcome — shorter DUP = better prognosis. Refer immediately to psychiatry. Antipsychotics should be started at the lowest effective dose. Involve family. Address substance use. Naturopathic role: sleep, nutrition, omega-3 support, reduce substance use — always as adjuncts under psychiatric supervision.

cards.title: Bipolar Disorder — Recognition & Naturopathic Adjunct

alerts.label: Bipolar Disorder — Medical Management Essential ★
alerts.text: Mood stabilisers (lithium, valproate, lamotrigine) and antipsychotics are the mainstay — never replace with naturopathic treatment alone. Naturopathic role: adjunct support, medication side effect management, lifestyle stabilisation.

rows: Type I = full mania; Type II = hypomania only

pearls.label: 🟡 Misdiagnosis Pearl
pearls.text: Bipolar disorder is misdiagnosed as unipolar depression in 40% of cases — average delay to correct diagnosis is 10 years. Red flags for bipolar in a "depressed" patient: family history of bipolar, onset before 25, hypomanic episodes after antidepressants (antidepressant-induced mania), seasonal pattern, psychotic features during depression, history of impulsive behaviour. Always screen with MDQ (Mood Disorder Questionnaire) before prescribing St John's Wort or stimulating supplements for depression.

steps.title: Omega-3 Fatty Acids
steps.text: EPA+DHA 2–4g/day — strongest naturopathic evidence for bipolar depression. EPA is the active anti-depressant component. Multiple RCTs show significant reduction in depressive (not manic) symptoms as adjunct to mood stabilisers. Start low, increase slowly.

steps.title: Circadian Stabilisation ★
steps.text: Irregular sleep-wake cycles are the #1 trigger for mood episodes. Prescribe: fixed sleep/wake times 7 days/week, morning bright light therapy (10min), no blue light after 8pm, avoid shift work and jet lag where possible. Social rhythm therapy (SRT) is evidence-based psychosocial intervention.

steps.title: Lithium Side Effect Support
steps.text: Lithium causes: tremor (Mg 400mg/day helps), polyuria/polydipsia (monitor renal function), hypothyroidism (check TSH 6-monthly, supplement if needed), weight gain (low-GI diet, exercise). Never advise stopping lithium — abrupt cessation triggers severe rebound mania.

steps.title: Avoid in Bipolar
steps.text: St John's Wort — can trigger mania in bipolar patients. High-dose stimulating adaptogens (Rhodiola, Ginseng) during euthymic/depressive phase — switch risk. Caffeine excess — disrupts sleep, lowers seizure threshold. Alcohol — destabilises mood, interacts with medications.

cards.title: ADHD — Assessment & Naturopathic Protocol

alerts.label: ADHD — Neurodevelopmental Disorder
alerts.text: ADHD is a neurodevelopmental condition with neurobiological basis (dopamine/noradrenaline dysregulation, frontal-striatal circuit differences). It is NOT caused by poor parenting or diet alone — but nutrition, sleep, and lifestyle significantly modulate symptom severity.

rows: Difficulty sustaining attention, easily distracted, forgetful, loses things
Often missed in girls — "daydreaming", academic underperformance
Fidgeting, inability to sit still, talks excessively, impulsive decisions
Classic presentation — often diagnosed in boys
Most adults with ADHD have combined type
Dopamine synthesis cofactor, methylphenidate response modulator
Dopamine synthesis — ferritin <30 worsens ADHD significantly
Remove: artificial colours, preservatives, gluten (if sensitive), dairy
★★★ Good — Southampton study confirmed food dyes worsen ADHD
★★★★ Strong — equivalent to low-dose methylphenidate acutely

pearls.label: 🟡 Ferritin & ADHD
pearls.text: Check serum ferritin in ALL children with ADHD before starting supplements. Ferritin <30 µg/L is associated with significantly worse ADHD symptoms — independent of anaemia. Iron is an essential cofactor for tyrosine hydroxylase (the rate-limiting enzyme in dopamine synthesis). Correcting iron deficiency alone improves ADHD scores comparably to low-dose stimulants in some children. Target ferritin >50 µg/L. Use iron bisglycinate — better absorbed, less GI upset than ferrous sulphate.

cards.title: Eating Disorders — Recognition & Naturopathic Support

alerts.label: Eating Disorders — Highest Mortality of Any Psychiatric Disorder ★
alerts.text: Anorexia nervosa has the highest mortality rate of any psychiatric condition (5–10%). Medical stabilisation and specialist psychiatric/dietitian care are ESSENTIAL — naturopathic role is adjunct support only. Never manage eating disorders without specialist team involvement.

rows: BMI <17.5, fear of weight gain, distorted body image, restriction
Cardiac arrhythmia (QTc prolongation), electrolyte imbalance, osteoporosis, refeeding syndrome
Nutritional rehabilitation support, bone health, REFER specialist
Binge-purge cycles, normal/near-normal weight, dental erosion, Russell's sign
Hypokalaemia (arrhythmia), oesophagitis, parotid enlargement, electrolyte imbalance

Metabolic syndrome, T2DM, cardiovascular risk, depression
Mindful eating, emotional regulation, gut health, blood sugar stabilisation

pearls.label: 🟡 Orthorexia — The Hidden Eating Disorder
pearls.text: Orthorexia nervosa (not yet in DSM) = obsessive focus on eating "correctly" or "healthily" — causes significant distress, nutritional deficiency, and social impairment. Common in health-conscious populations and IRONICALLY in patients seeking naturopathic care. Red flags: extreme food rules, anxiety when rules are broken, elimination of entire food groups without medical reason, social withdrawal around food. The naturopathic community has a responsibility to model a balanced, flexible relationship with food — not rigid dietary perfectionism.

steps.title: Red Flags — Refer Immediately
steps.text: BMI <15, rapid weight loss (>1kg/week), cardiac symptoms (palpitations, syncope, chest pain), electrolyte abnormalities (K <3.0, Na <130), refusal to eat/drink, severe depression or suicidal ideation. These require hospital admission — not outpatient naturopathic care.

steps.title: Nutritional Support in Recovery
steps.text: Zinc 30mg/day — deficiency worsens anorexia and appetite suppression (zinc reduces appetite in deficiency). Omega-3 3g/day — reduces depression and anxiety common in eating disorders. Vit D + Ca + K2 — critical for bone density (osteoporosis affects 50% of anorexia patients). B-complex for metabolic support during refeeding.

cards.title: Burnout — Clinical Assessment & Recovery Protocol

alerts.label: Burnout — WHO ICD-11 (2019)
alerts.text: Burnout is an occupational phenomenon — NOT a medical condition. Defined by 3 dimensions: (1) Emotional exhaustion — feeling depleted, drained. (2) Depersonalisation/cynicism — detachment, negative attitudes. (3) Reduced sense of accomplishment — feeling ineffective.

rows: Irritability, reduced efficiency, sleep disturbance, recurrent illness
Adaptogens, Mg, sleep priority, workload reduction
Chronic fatigue, cynicism, anxiety, physical symptoms (GI, headache, palpitations)
Complete exhaustion, detachment, cognitive impairment, depression overlap
Medical leave essential + full HPA rehabilitation

steps.title: Assessment Tools
steps.text: Maslach Burnout Inventory (MBI) — gold standard, 22 items, 3 subscales. Copenhagen Burnout Inventory (CBI) — free, validated. Distinguish from depression (PHQ-9): burnout is context-specific (work) and improves with vacation; depression is pervasive.

steps.title: HPA Rehabilitation Protocol
steps.text: Sleep 8–9hrs — non-negotiable first step. Ashwagandha KSM-66 300mg BD (cortisol ↓ in stage 1–2; adrenal support in stage 3–4). Rhodiola rosea 200–400mg AM (fatigue and cognitive performance — avoid in stage 4 low cortisol). Vit C 1–2g BD (adrenal cortex support). Pantothenic acid (B5) 500mg — cortisol synthesis cofactor. Magnesium glycinate 400mg night.

steps.title: Lifestyle Restructuring
steps.text: Work reduction — minimum 20% workload reduction in stage 3–4 (medical certificate if employed). Exercise: yoga and walking ONLY in stage 4 (HIIT worsens HPA). Nature exposure 2hrs/week (cortisol ↓ 12% — Shinrin-yoku). Digital detox — scheduled screen-free time. Restore social connection — isolation worsens burnout.

steps.title: Distinguish from Depression
steps.text: Key difference: in burnout, patient says "I have nothing left to give"; in depression, "I am nothing." Burnout → depression is a real progression — screen with PHQ-9 at every visit. If PHQ-9 >10, refer for psychological support. Burnout + depression = significantly worse prognosis without dual treatment.

alerts.text: ERP (Exposure and Response Prevention) = gold standard. SSRIs at higher doses. NAC 2400–3000mg/day (glutamate modulator — RCT evidence). Inositol 12–18g/day. NEVER provide repeated reassurance — it worsens OCD (reassurance IS a compulsion).

pearls.label: 🟡 Grief vs Depression — Distinction
pearls.text: Normal grief: waves of sadness triggered by reminders, preserved self-esteem, short-lived low mood, person can experience positive emotions. Complicated grief (Prolonged Grief Disorder): yearning, difficulty accepting loss, bitterness, intrusive images >12 months — impairs functioning. Major depression: pervasive hopelessness, anhedonia, worthlessness, vegetative symptoms — requires antidepressant ± therapy. Social support, grief groups, narrative therapy, ritual, and meaning-making are the primary naturopathic tools for uncomplicated grief. Never pathologise normal grief.

pearls.label: 🟡 Never Provide Reassurance in OCD
pearls.text: Reassurance WORSENS OCD — it is a form of compulsion that reinforces the cycle. In OCD, the therapeutic approach is the opposite of what patients want: "stop performing the compulsion even though the anxiety feels unbearable." This is ERP — habituation occurs when the compulsion is resisted. Do not ask "are you sure the door was locked?" alongside a patient — you are reinforcing their checking behaviour. Educate family members on this critical point.

steps.title: Evidence-Based Treatment — Refer
steps.text: ERP (Exposure and Response Prevention — CBT) is gold standard — superior to medication alone. SSRIs (fluoxetine, fluvoxamine, clomipramine) — effective in 60%, higher doses needed than for depression. Refer to OCD-specialist therapist — generic CBT therapists may not be trained in ERP.

steps.title: Naturopathic Adjunct
steps.text: NAC (N-Acetyl Cysteine) 2400-3000mg/day — glutamate modulator, reduces compulsive behaviours (RCT evidence for OCD and trichotillomania). Inositol 12-18g/day — competes with serotonin receptor signalling, comparable to fluvoxamine in one trial. Omega-3 3g/day — anti-neuroinflammatory. Exercise 30 min daily — serotonin and BDNF up.

pearls.label: 🟡 Supporting Bereaved Patients
pearls.text: What NOT to say: "They are in a better place", "Everything happens for a reason", "Be strong", "I know how you feel", "At least they had a good life." What TO say: "I am so sorry for your loss", "I cannot imagine how hard this must be", "Tell me about them." Practical help: offer specific tasks ("I will bring dinner Tuesday") rather than vague offers. Grief does not follow stages in order (Kubler-Ross stages are commonly misapplied). The naturopathic therapeutic relationship — consistent, compassionate, non-judgmental presence — is itself a healing intervention.

steps.title: Evidence-Based Trauma Therapies — Refer
steps.text: EMDR (Eye Movement Desensitisation and Reprocessing) — WHO first-line. Trauma-focused CBT. Prolonged Exposure. Somatic Experiencing (Peter Levine). All require trained specialist therapists.

steps.title: Mind-Body Naturopathic Adjuncts
steps.text: Trauma-sensitive yoga reduces PTSD symptom scores significantly (van der Kolk RCT). HRV biofeedback — reduces hyperarousal, improves parasympathetic tone. MBSR 8-week programme reduces PTSD symptoms 30-40%. Extended exhalation breathing (4:6 ratio) activates vagus nerve, reduces hyperarousal. Acupuncture — comparable to CBT in military veteran RCTs.

alerts.label: Addiction — Medical Supervision Essential for Withdrawal
alerts.text: Withdrawal from alcohol, benzodiazepines, and opioids can be fatal. Never manage withdrawal without medical supervision. Naturopathic role is in recovery support — not acute detoxification.

cards.title: Autism Spectrum Disorder (ASD) — Naturopathic

alerts.label: BNYS Role in ASD — Supportive, Not Curative
alerts.text: ASD is a neurodevelopmental condition — not caused by vaccines (Andrew Wakefield's fraudulent 1998 paper was fully retracted in 2010). Naturopathic support targets: comorbidities (GI dysfunction, sleep disorders, anxiety, immune dysregulation) which significantly affect function and quality of life. Always work within a multidisciplinary team: developmental paediatrician, speech therapy, applied behaviour analysis (ABA), occupational therapy.

rows: Gut microbiome optimisation: probiotics (Lactobacillus rhamnosus + Bifidobacterium longum ★★). Dietary fibre (challenging — sensory issues with texture). Gluten-casein free diet: equivocal evidence overall, but subset with genuine food sensitivity shows significant behavioural improvement. Trial 3–6 months with dietitian.

Melatonin 0.5–5mg ★★★ (most evidence-based ASD supplement — Cochrane: significant improvement in sleep onset and total sleep time in ASD). Sleep hygiene protocol adapted for sensory needs: weighted blanket, consistent routine, blackout curtains, white noise.

Mind-body: yoga ★★ (adapted yoga for ASD improves anxiety, sensory processing, social interaction). Magnesium + B6 combination ★★ (Rimland protocol: high-dose B6 200–500mg + Mg 100–200mg — used extensively in ASD research). HRV biofeedback ★★.

Omega-3 DHA 1–2g/day ★★ (reduces hyperactivity and stereotypy in ASD RCTs). Vit D 2000–3000 IU (deficiency common — VDR dysfunction in ASD). NAC 900–1800mg/day ★★ (reduces irritability and repetitive behaviours in RCT).

pearls.label: 🟡 Sensory Processing in ASD — Practical Clinical Adjustments
pearls.text: ASD patients may have: hypersensitivity to touch (light touch more aversive than deep pressure — use firm handshake, not light touch). Auditory hypersensitivity (loud clinic environment may cause meltdown — quiet room, avoid paging systems during appointment). Visual sensitivity (fluorescent lighting may cause distress — use natural light or warm lighting). Many ASD patients prefer: consistent appointment timing and structure, advance notice of any change, written or visual instructions over verbal-only. Adjust your consultation environment and communication style accordingly. Parents/carers are invaluable informants — include them in every ASD consultation.

cards.title: Personality Disorders — Recognition

alerts.label: Personality Disorders in Naturopathic Practice
alerts.text: Personality disorders are pervasive, enduring patterns of inner experience and behaviour that deviate from cultural expectations, are inflexible, and cause distress or impairment. They significantly affect the therapeutic relationship, treatment adherence, and clinical outcomes. Recognition helps adapt communication and set appropriate boundaries.

rows: Paranoid: mistrust, suspicion of others' motives. Schizoid: detachment from relationships, restricted emotion. Schizotypal: eccentric behaviour, odd beliefs, perceptual distortions.
May be suspicious of medical interventions or perceive judgement where none exists. Require explicit, transparent communication. Avoid jargon.
Intense, unstable relationships, identity disturbance, impulsivity, self-harm, fear of abandonment, emotional dysregulation. "All-or-nothing" thinking (splitting).

Most clinically impactful: may idealise then devalue practitioner ("best doctor ever" → "you don't care about me"). Clear, consistent boundaries are ESSENTIAL. Refer to DBT-trained therapist. Document all consultations thoroughly.
Grandiosity, need for admiration, lack of empathy. Entitlement. Exploitative.
May demand special treatment, become angry when given standard care. Complaining and litigation risk higher. Maintain equanimity and professional distance.
Disregard for others' rights, deceit, impulsivity, aggressiveness, lawbreaking. Callousness.
May attempt to manipulate practitioner (seeking medications, false symptoms). Firm, non-judgmental boundaries. Do not be manipulated into inappropriate prescribing.
Avoidant: inhibition, sensitivity to criticism, feeling inadequate. Dependent: excessive need to be cared for, clinging. OCPD: preoccupation with order, perfectionism, control.
Avoidant: may avoid follow-up out of fear of evaluation. Dependent: may over-rely on practitioner, become distressed by boundaries. OCPD: may resist natural, "uncontrolled" approaches.

pearls.label: 🟡 BPD — The Most Important Cluster B for BNYS
pearls.text: Borderline personality disorder (BPD) is more common in clinical settings than general population (5–10% of outpatients). BNYS practitioners should: set clear boundaries at the beginning of the relationship (session length, communication channels, what constitutes an emergency). Use consistent, warm, non-reactive responses to splitting. Refer to: DBT (Dialectical Behaviour Therapy) specialist — the gold-standard treatment for BPD ★★★. ACT and schema therapy also effective. Never attempt to provide intensive psychotherapy within a naturopathic consultation — this creates unhealthy dependency and is outside BNYS scope. Having a named psychiatrist co-managing a BPD patient significantly reduces practitioner burden.

cards.title: Pharmacology of Psychiatric Drugs

alerts.label: Why BNYS Practitioners Need Psychiatric Drug Knowledge
alerts.text: Most BNYS patients are on or have been on psychiatric medications. Understanding mechanisms, side effects, nutritional interactions, and tapering principles enables: (1) Appropriate herb-drug interaction screening. (2) Managing medication side effects naturopathically. (3) Supporting patients who wish to taper under medical supervision. (4) Communicating effectively with prescribing physicians.

rows: GI nausea (first 2 weeks), sexual dysfunction (50%), insomnia or sedation, weight gain (paroxetine worst), discontinuation syndrome (especially paroxetine)

St John's Wort: ABSOLUTE contraindication (serotonin syndrome risk ★★★). Omega-3 ★★★ (augments SSRI in depression). Folate/methylfolate ★★★ (augments — reduces non-response). Magnesium (reduces side effects). Reduce antidepressant dose by 50% cautiously — always under medical supervision
Hypertension (↑ BP at higher doses — especially venlafaxine). Sexual dysfunction. Sweating. Difficult discontinuation (venlafaxine has severe discontinuation syndrome — must taper 10% per month)
Same cautions as SSRI. Duloxetine: also treats DPN and fibromyalgia. Never stop SNRIs abruptly.

Sedation, dry mouth, constipation, urinary retention, orthostatic hypotension, weight gain, cardiac arrhythmia (QT prolongation — dangerous in overdose → never prescribe large quantities to suicidal patients)
Avoid: anticholinergic herbs (belladonna). Alcohol potentiates sedation dramatically. CoQ10 (mitochondrial protection from TCA oxidative stress). TCAs deplete: CoQ10, B2, B12, folate.
EPS (extrapyramidal symptoms): acute dystonia, akathisia (inner restlessness), parkinsonism. Tardive dyskinesia with long-term use. NMS (neuroleptic malignant syndrome) = rare but fatal emergency.
CoQ10 200mg + Vit E 400IU (reduce EPS severity ★★). Ginkgo biloba 120mg reduces TD severity ★★. B6 (pyridoxine or P5P) 300mg reduces TD and akathisia ★★★ (strong evidence).

Metabolic syndrome (olanzapine/clozapine worst — weight gain 5–10kg, T2DM risk 3×, dyslipidaemia, HTN). Sedation. Prolactin elevation (risperidone). Agranulocytosis (clozapine — mandatory monitoring).

Clozapine + St John's Wort: ↓ clozapine levels (CYP3A4). Omega-3 3g (reduces metabolic side effects, augments risperidone ★★). Metformin (medical) for antipsychotic-induced metabolic syndrome. Berberine (naturopathic metabolic protection).
Varied: GSK3β inhibition (Li), Na channel (CBZ/LTG), GABA potentiation (VPA)

Lithium: thyroid (hypothyroidism, requires monitoring), renal (nephrogenic DI), tremor, polyuria, teratogen. Valproate: teratogen (spina bifida), PCOS-like ovarian cysts, hepatotoxic, weight gain. CBZ: agranulocytosis, SJS.

Lithium: NSAIDs, diuretics ↑ lithium levels → toxicity (★★★ critical drug interaction). Maintain Na+ intake (low-salt diet → lithium retention). Valproate depletes: carnitine, selenium, zinc, folate — supplement all. CBZ: CYP inducer reduces many drug/herb levels.
